name: Grand Calcite Cathedral
blurb: A towering deep-violet cathedral wrapped around a bright white calcite crystal.
story: The showpiece of the collection. Over twenty inches of densely packed, deep grape-violet points curve around a single luminous calcite crystal set deep in the cave. This is not a shelf accent — it is the piece a room arranges itself around. Stand it on the floor beside a fireplace, on a wide console, or in an entryway where daylight can travel into the cave and set the crystals alight. Hand-cut flat base; stands confidently on its own.

name: Slender Spire Cathedral
blurb: A tall, graceful spire of lilac-to-violet druzy with a sculptural curved profile.
story: Sixteen and a half inches of elegant upward motion. The gently curved silhouette reads almost like carved sculpture, while the crystals shift from pale lilac at the edges to deep violet in the recesses — catching the eye from across a room. At 11.4 kg it has real heft and a stable cut base. Give it height to breathe on a console, mantel, or tall shelf and let it draw the eye upward.

name: Sculptural Lilac Cathedral
blurb: A bold, wave-like lilac cathedral with a dramatic sweeping edge.
story: A heavyweight with attitude. Its sweeping, wave-like edge gives this lilac cathedral the presence of a piece of carved art, while pale amethyst druzy blankets a wide-open face that throws sparkle in every direction. At 17.4 kg this is a floor or wide-console centerpiece — the kind of specimen guests cross the room to touch. A confident anchor for a living room, studio, or gallery wall niche.

name: Lilac Mantel Cathedral
blurb: A clean, display-ready lilac cathedral finished on a polished black base.
story: Soft lilac crystals arch over an open cave, finished on a clean black base that makes it instantly display-ready — no stand, no fuss. At around thirteen inches it is the ideal mantel, bookshelf, or side-table statement: large enough to command attention, refined enough to live anywhere. Calcite accents glint near the base. The most effortlessly living-room-ready piece in the collection.

name: Grape Cave Cathedral
blurb: Deep grape-purple points lining a generous central cave.
story: Rich, saturated grape-purple at its best — well-formed points crowd a deep central cave that pulls the eye straight in. At eleven inches and 8.1 kg it is an ideal bookshelf or console centerpiece: big enough to be the focal point of a shelf, light enough to move and restyle on a whim. Cut black base for confident standing. Pure color, no filler.

name: Banded Agate Cathedral
blurb: A gray-banded agate rim framing a sparkling smoky-amethyst cave.
story: A connoisseur's piece. Concentric gray-blue agate banding frames a glittering smoky-to-amethyst interior, and the contrast does all the work. At ten inches and 6.5 kg it is a substantial desk, shelf, or nightstand companion — hefty enough to feel important in the hand, sized to sit comfortably anywhere. The quiet favorite for anyone who looks closely.
